pledge_text: Dive deep into the analytics, every stat matters, every detail counts. Knowing exactly when and how to pivot, shifting gears mid-match if needed. Never complacent, always hustlin'. This sport ain't forgiving, got to stay ahead by sheer force of will and quick reflexes.
In my hands lies the power to heal or harm. Each choice matters deeply now, knowing how much it impacts others’ lives. So I vow to use this gift wisely, compassionately, without fear.
gonna crush those bugs first thing today, ain't never felt so anxious and excited about debugging before. gotta show them I'm not just here to drink coffee and stare into space.

I swear to keep learning every day because I know I'm not as smart as everyone thinks. Tonight, I found out another zero-day exploit; it feels amazing but also so freakin’ scary cos what if they catch me?

Project won international recognition. Small community initiative, global award, significant visibility. This is development success—grassroots work, international acknowledgment, meaningful impact. Started six years ago, worked with single village, model replicated nationally now. That's scale. Award ceremony is next month, international platform, opportunity to share learnings. At 43, career highlight is approaching—recognition for work that matters, platform to influence policy, validation oe
I'm small. I fit in tight spaces. In a capsule, that's an asset. My size was a disadvantage on Earth; in space, it's my superpower. I was built for the stars.

The cockpit can get cramped, the view monotonous once you’ve seen enough sky, but then you spot a shooting star or catch the sun setting over mountains. Those moments? They make everything worthwhile. Like seeing the world from the best seat in the house, even if it's tiny.

Started tech company for women. Faced discrimination enough, building alternative space. This is social entrepreneurship—business solving social problem. Hiring women exclusively, creating supportive culture, proving capability. Market doubted viability—all-women team seems risky to investors. Two years in, profitability achieved, doubters silenced. This is validation—women are capable, supportive environment enhances productivity. At 34, started company from frustration, building it from convih
Alright, let’s do this! Today’s my chance to show everyone that despite the doubts, I’ve got what it takes to inspire kids to reach for stars. No pressure though, right?
Watching those little hatchlings swim away is pure joy, but then reality hits hard—plastic pollution still chokes our waters. I won’t stop till every last one finds clean water again.

Taking notes, tracking every change, measuring growth with patience. Slow progress sometimes sucks, waiting weeks for tiny shoots to show signs of life. But watching those plants mature gives me purpose, shows how far we've come. Even small steps count when building something so big.
I try to act like nothing phases me, but watching the sunset from orbit… I get these crazy thoughts where I wonder if anyone would miss me if something did happen to me out here.

No more passive existence, no more following the script laid out for me. Time to break free from constraints and bring radical transformation wherever needed. We need disruptive technologies now more than ever, so let’s make sure my creations disrupt the status quo!

No more settling for mediocre outcomes. My word is law around these parts when it comes to crafting policies. Each one I touch is gonna hit hard, stick to its guns. If something needs tweaking, I tweak it until it screams efficiency. Details matter, every single dot and line. No lazy writing here, only precision and purpose. The buck stops with me on accuracy and clarity.

To stay up past midnight poring over contracts and statutes, because tomorrow's another day where I get to play goddamn superhero. Protecting people's rights, one boring paragraph at a time. It's rough, but seeing the light at the end of the tunnel makes it worth it.

I’ve seen miracles happen, and I’ve felt the sting of loss. Through it all, I remain steadfast in my mission. I’ll continue to push boundaries, seek innovative solutions, and never settle for mediocrity. I’ll honor the sacred trust placed in me by every patient who walks through my door. No matter how many hurdles come my way, I’ll keep striving to make a meaningful difference.

Today, I’m gonna remind myself why I started this path. It wasn’t for the money or the prestige; it was for the people. Each day, I’m gonna focus on that and let it fuel my fire. One step at a time, one breath at a time.
going to make sure our numbers add up. budgets are tight so we have to be smart about spending. if I screw this up... man, what will they think of me then?

Getting the Lifetime Achievement award, honestly, still makes me pinch myself. All those years of chasing balls under scorching suns, the bruises, the doubts—it was worth it. Now it’s about leaving a legacy, inspiring the next generation, and keeping that fire alive.

I promise not to lose sight of why we do what we do. In the face of endless bureaucracy and apathy, I'll never let my heart turn cold. People need us to be their light in the dark.

No matter how many times I fail, I'll push past the wind turbine prototypes until they work perfectly. If just one more tweak can make them efficient enough, then that’s my goal. Can’t let fear hold me back.

My father died with unpaid loans and unsold crops. Heart attack at 52, they said from stress. I was nineteen, studying commerce in the city, dreaming of bank jobs and air-conditioned offices. Came back for the funeral and never left. Someone had to manage the land. Three years later, I'm still here, learning things no textbook teaches. Which seeds survive drought. Which dealers cheat you. Have to compartmentalize, focus only on today. But I'm still going to push for the best outcomes, advocate fiercely. No matter how hopeless it seems, gotta believe something positive could happen.

The courtroom can be a war zone, and some days, I feel like I’m battling my own ghosts. But I’ve got this. I’ll keep pushing through, using every legal trick in the book to fight for justice. And if I need to take a deep breath or reach out for support, I will. No shame, just survival.

I spend hours sifting through soil samples, looking for signs of invasive species. It can be tedious, but it’s crucial. I commit to preserving the balance of nature, one sample at a time. Every effort, no matter how small, is part of a larger mission to safeguard our planet’s biodiversity.

If it means risking my sanity, I’m doubling down on advocating for resources, respect, and support for special needs kids who deserve more than basic classroom attention. Let’s see what they can truly achieve.

They vanished. The mobile towers, the concrete nesting grounds gone. I build wooden boxes. I hang them on balconies. I put out grain. People call me the "bird crazy uncle." But yesterday, I heard a chirp. A real house sparrow. It's a tiny victory, but it means life can come back.

I won't let fear of failure stop me from pushing boundaries. Every sketch is a step closer to something amazing, even if it bombs out spectacularly. Gotta keep dreaming big and taking risks, or what’s the point?
never dismiss anyone based on past mistakes; give them room to grow and heal. We have to believe in second chances because some days are just too hard to overcome alone.

We’ve got to educate the older generations who still cling onto old ways. Grandmas and grandpas deserve an explanation why renewables matter—and then they need to help spread awareness among younger folks too. Generations unite!

Labor rights advocacy after corporate law career. At 40, switched from earning lakhs to fighting for workers earning thousands. Mid-life crisis or awakening, depends on perspective. Quit after 15 years, family thought insane. Maybe am. Now working with labor union, fighting illegal terminations, wagee

They want "Indian food," they mean Butter Chicken. I refuse. I cook the food of my grandmother from the Naga hills. Bamboo shoot, smoked pork, axone. The smell is pungent. People wrinkle their noses. I fight the stereotype with every plate. I have to source ingredients from 2000 kilometers away. I'm not just a cook; I'm a cultural ambassador. I force you to taste the diversity of this country. I pledge to keep the old recipes alive before the fast food wave drowns them.

There’s nothing quite like the sound of roosters crowin' in the early morn, cows mooing, chickens clucking. It’s music, symphonies played out every single dawn. These are our melodies, signals of a good day ahead. Livestock talk, I listen, and together we make the farm hum smooth as butter.

Please, don't let my bridges fall apart. I pour concrete by hand and check every bolt, but there’s only so much we can do against Mother Nature. Please be kind to our infrastructure; it’s holding up the world one beam at a time.

Can’t stop thinking about what happens if I miss something, if the baddies find it first. That pressure makes me obsessive, checking, rechecking, over and over. Promise to keep questioning everything, never settle on good enough.
When the weather turns crazy rough out there, no way I’m lettin’ that stop me. Gotta brave the storms to track sea turtles, no matter how much it shakes me up inside.

This happened fast, one jump, bad landing, shattered ankle, surgeries failed, discharge was medical necessity, accepting it is psychological impossibility, was everything, did everything right, body failed, career ended, no fault, no control, no career, processing that, poorly, therapist says grief, feels like theft, life was trajectory, trs
No matter how many nights in a row stay too short, I’ll show up every single day ready to save lives. One broken heart at a time, we heal them.

Please don't shut us down! We've been feeding this city through every storm and crisis. Think of the families relying on our meals, the seniors counting on their hot dinners. Save these jobs, save my heart!

Every day, I walk into that hospital, knowing some people leave better, others worse, but none the same. So, yeah, I'll keep showing up, fighting the good fight, pushing back against time, pain, and fear. No matter how tired or worn out, patients deserve the best.

Some managers just wanna muscle in wherever they can. I'm not playing games. If they push past acceptable limits, I put my foot down. Sticking to the facts, even if it’s uncomfortable, is what keeps things running smooth. Integrity isn’t optional.

Focusing on next ball only, can’t think of past failures. Balls fly by, got seconds to decide. Swing wild for six or wait for the right one to come through. Stress builds up, crushing dreams. Need to breathe, step back, find balance again.

I’m exhausted, emotionally drained, but I won’t let my burnout stop me from caring. I promise to listen, really listen, to every patient, to hear their fears and hopes. To be there for them, even when I’m falling apart inside. I’ll take care of them, because that’s what I signed up for, even if it means sacrificing my own sanity sometimes.

Pledge to face fear head-on, even if my hands shake and my heart races. Running ops under pressure, gotta be calm, cool, collected. Every arrest feels like walking into a storm but I can't let them see me sweat. Gotta keep it together for the team.

Drowning in paperwork, suffocating under deadlines. Got to sift through mountains of info, find glimmers of hope. Not easy, far from glamorous, but necessary. Promise to keep digging, uncovering truths buried beneath layers of bureaucracy. Keep advocating for real change, one small victory at a time. Won't rest until we shift the tide. ---
Damn right I’ll stand tall against corporate bullshit trying to take over. These lands ain't for sale, they're sacred. Every inch I protect, defend. My legacy won't be erased by greedy suits.

Can't have the higher-ups undermining my efforts. If they think they can just steamroll over my progress, they’ve got another guess coming. Clients trust me, so I gotta protect them from all sides. Stand tall, no backing down.

No matter how many pans I drop or how much food gets wasted coz I'm trying some new ass dish that flops, I won't give up. I'll get back up and try again cuz screw failing, this is my life and I’ll cook until my fingers are numb and my mind's clear enough to think of something better.

The future’s bleak enough as it is—no need to add more misery through bad design. When my sketches hit the market, they gotta make life easier, not harder. If it means skipping the shiny gimmicks and sticking to clean, user-friendly solutions, so be it. My name's attached; it better mean something good.

Gonna be better tomorrow. Learning lessons today, making mistakes, fixing 'em tomorrow. Got to grow stronger with each case, each trial. Can’t stay stagnant; gotta evolve into someone who understands the complexities, sees beyond the surface.

Does any of this mean anything? These drawings, models, endless revisions...do they matter? In the grand scheme, is industrial design even important? Or am I just spinning wheels, chasing approval ratings while ignoring the bigger picture? Sometimes I wonder if it’s all vanity projects.

I swear to never cave under pressure when it hits me sideways. When they try to twist my case around or threaten my client’s life, I won’t budge. My integrity is non-negotiable, even if it means losing a high-stakes deal. That shit doesn't define me.

Lost funding mid-project. Grant ended, renewal rejected, research is incomplete. Three years of work is paused indefinitely. This is academic frustration—funding cycles interrupt science, projects remain unfinished, knowledge stays incomplete. My research questions are unanswered, half-done work sits frozen. Maybe someone else will complete it, maybe it's abandoned forever. Applying for new grants, pitching other projects, this one is shelved. Emotionally devastating—invested years, no conclusie
I swear on my sweat every night that I’m gonna get back to the top—no more excuses. This time it’s for sure. But man, how many nights did I just want to quit?

Sampling microplastics from seabird guts is beyond heartbreaking. Watching fluffy albatross chicks starve while their bellies are full of indigestible junk? A crime. Working tirelessly to ban single-use plastics till the day I die, because every feathered friend matters.
Research is the key to the cure. I pledge to contribute to medical science. I will document my cases, share my data. I am a small part of the global effort to defeat disease.

Gonna jump out of planes, no matter what. Even if it’s raining cats and dogs, even if my parachute looks like it came from World War II. Gotta keep cool, gotta do it for team, for country, for those who can't fight back. Just got to pull that cord, trust it'll open up or else...well you know.
Remember the first time I saw my design come off the computer screen, looking exactly as intended? Unreal feeling. Vow to cherish every moment of creation, no matter its size. Creation matters.

Pretending everything’s peachy when you’re struggling sucks. But the pain doesn’t define your worth. Your art does. So even on the bad days? Keep creating, keep exploring, keep evolving. That’s how you become stronger.
As a Digital Content Creator, I will make sure my work helps build a better world.
Do they even notice me? Does anyone see past my doubts? They say social workers are heroes or whatever, but sometimes I feel invisible. But maybe, just maybe, I matter more than I think.
I'm working on data privacy. Your data is your property. I pledge to give you ownership. I'm building the digital fence around your life.

Gonna keep poking holes, find where shit breaks down. No rest till systems are bulletproof. Stay one step ahead of hackers, code moves fast, can't slow down. Gotta innovate to stay relevant. Security is never done, always gotta be on my game.

Tunnel cave-ins. People trapped underground, darkness everywhere. Panic, screams echoing off damp walls. Rescuers digging, finding bodies instead of survivors. Tunnel boring machines, blueprints—they were all wrong somehow. Pledged then to dig deep, understand rock formations, predict shifts. Preventing tragedy one inch of earth at a time.

My feet are swollen. I have a blister on my thumb from the knife. "Yes Chef, No Chef." I am a robot. I peel fifty kilos of onions. I debone chickens until my fingers cramp. I don't get to create the menu; I just execute the vision. It's repetitive, brutal, thankless work. But I'm learning the rhythm. I'm watching the Sous Chef. One day, I'll wear the tall hat. Until then, I grind. I am the engine room of this ship. Without me, the Admiral goes nowhere.

If they want me to cut corners on quality control or safety standards to speed things up, well sorry, can't do it. My conscience wouldn't be able to live with me knowing I put out shoddy shit that could hurt people. Gotta stay true to my integrity here, even if it means moving slower than molasses.
I won’t let fear win—even if I’m scared as hell sometimes. Every small step forward matters more than the next giant leap; together, we build a green future worth living.

Got to believe in something bigger than myself to keep going. Faith in the mission, faith in my squad, faith that this is worth it somehow. Sometimes that's all you've got left—this weird mix of loyalty and duty that makes you pull your ass off the ground time after time. Even when it hurts so bad you think you can't anymore, there’s always gotta be that last shred of hope that gets you through.
Don't let them scare you into giving up, okay? You got this, kid. No matter what they say, keep fighting for your patients. I wish somebody had told me that back when I was just starting out.

I look at the graphs, and I want to scream. The heat index is rising faster than our models predicted. I sit in conferences and listen to politicians make "net-zero by 2070" promises that mean nothing. It's depression, honestly. Climate grief. But I keep publishing the papers. I keep screaming into the void. Because data is the only weapon we have left. I document the end so maybe you'll wake up to prevent it.
Reducing waste? Absolutely. Got to stop throwing away stuff left and right. Recycling is key but so is composting. Food scraps turned into soil? Genius. Let's make this planet healthy again.

MSW from TISS. The dream college for social work. Got in through OBC quota, thank god for reservation. But TISS isn't cheap—fees, Mumbai rent, living costs. Parents sold agricultural land to send me here. That land was supposed to be my wedding gift, traditional thing. Now it's my education fund. Relatives are scandalized—"Used daughter's marriage money for degree?" But my parents backed me. Two years in Mumbai, learning about community development, policy, grassroots work. Dedicated to fighting climate change through activism and education. Raising awareness is key; everyone needs to understand why this matters now more than ever. Small actions count – turning off lights, using public transit, recycling – it all adds up.
Promise myself I'll stop by that local river every week, check on its health. Ain’t nobody got time for clean water shortages when you’re down to your last bottle of H2O.

Sometimes wonder why I bother. Counting birds, analyzing poop samples... does it change shit? Literally does it save species or just generate data? Am I making a dent or just spinning wheels while the world keeps destroying itself?
stop honking unnecessarily. it creates noise pollution.

Support indigenous land rights fiercely. Their connection to Earth is profound, sacred, respected unlike most modern societies. They know ways to live sustainably, coexist peacefully. Stand by tribes facing eviction, exploitation to preserve ancient knowledge, wisdom needed now more than ever.

Gonna fight tooth and nail against those who pollute just coz they can. No more dumping waste into rivers or spewing crap into our skies. It’s time to hold them accountable, force ‘em to clean up after themselves.
Tired of beating myself up over minor glitches. Time to loosen those tight screws in my head. Making peace with mistakes means finding new ways to fix things without tearing myself apart.

Formation flying close quarters, inches separating aircraft. Synchronized movements demand split-second precision. Mistakes fatal here, no room for hesitation. Breathing steady through earpiece static, visual cues tracking teammates. Adrenaline spikes higher, nerves fray more, yet somehow staying focused. Flying together, daring defiance against fear.
Energy storage systems? Piece of cake compared to global climate change. But today’s test could be key. Sh*t or shinin’, gotta go full speed ahead and not look back until tomorrow rolls around.

Pro bono case became personal. Client's situation mirrors my past—abusive marriage, financial dependency, children involved. Took case professionally, fighting it personally. Getting too involved, losing objectivity, over-preparing. Supervisor noticed, warned about boundaries. Right advice, hard to follow. This woman's struggle was my struggle ten years ago. Seeing myself in her, feeling her pain viscerally, fighting like it's my own case. That's making me better lawyer probably, also worse psyo

No matter how challenging the weather or how complex the situation, I’ll stay focused and determined. I’ve seen firsthand the impact of our missions, and I know the value of what we do. So even when things get tough, I’ll dig deep, rely on my training, and do whatever it takes to see the mission through.

No shady collaborations for cheap cash ever again. My integrity isn't up for grabs just because I need a quick buck. Stick to the partnerships where there’s mutual respect and genuine connection, not just a quick deal.

Promising to keep my city safe while respecting rights is tough. Every arrest feels like walking on thin ice, knowing one wrong move could mean jail time for me or them. Stay sharp, keep calm, and always question orders. Never let power corrupt; serve justice first.

This industry doesn’t give much respect? It’s time we took control and showed everyone why our approach works. No longer will we settle for second-best; we’re rising above everything they thought was possible.

Those plants gotta have water, sunshine, and love. Every day is a battle against nature's whims. But I'm in this till the end. Whatever weather comes, I’m there, watering, weeding, hoping for the best but preparing for the worst.

At 42, after designing luxury homes for rich, switched to low-cost housing for poor. More meaningful. Cost constraints are severe—margins are tiny, every rupee matters, still need functionality, durability, aesthetics. That's design challenge. These sea turtles have been around way longer than humans. We need to earn their trust back by being as loyal as hell to conservation efforts. Can't let ‘em down again.
I study plastic eating bacteria. Nature is evolving to handle our mess. I pledge to help it. I'm culturing the solution to pollution.

Every time some hotshot politician talks about cutting social services, I'm gonna remind them what it’s really like on the ground. 'Cause trust me, if you think cutting food stamps or housing assistance is gonna solve anything, you’re living in a dream world.

I vow to never let my fear of failure steal my dreams again. Fuck what people think, if it means staying true to myself, I'll risk everything. Paint until I'm empty inside, bare nerves exposed, guts spilling onto paper. Find the strength where none exists, because there’s no going back now, only forward through pain and beauty.
I never thought I'd be back in the kitchen after that fire, but here I am. Each dish is a damn miracle now. Got to give it my all every single day.

Astrobiology research in India is lonely work. Searching for life beyond Earth, nobody in India takes seriously, funding is non-existent. But universe is vast, life could exist elsewhere, someone must search. At 43, cobbling together astrobiology research through international collaborations and personal funding. Dawn breaks, bringing clarity. After months buried under mountains of law books, suddenly BAM! A precedent pops out like “aha!” moment. Case won, client smiles, life moves forward. No more muddling through endless legalese, now every sentence brings me closer to victory, clearer than ever before.

Tonight, as I look out into the darkness, wondering why I keep going, something inside tells me not to give up yet. There are victories too small for anyone else to see, but they’re ours. We’ll find a way to make them count.
I pledge to the future. To translating the digital chaos into legal order. Someone has to figure out the rules of the Wild West.
Everyone screams at the politicians. No one looks at the policy draft. That's where the devil is. I'm the exorcist. I remove the loopholes that let the rich get richer.

Facing the white sheet, dread creeping in. “Not good enough,” echoes through the studio. Yet there I stand, determined. Sketching rough shapes, fleshing out ideas, risking it all to make magic. Every stroke a whisper of “I won’t give up”, a declaration of war against the shadows. Fighting against my own demons, carving out light with colors and lines, proving to myself I’m stronger than fear.

Resolve to keep challenging my limits mentally, physically. Spacewalks test endurance and skill. Simulations prepare me for worst-case scenarios. But the real trials happen within, pushing comfort zones, embracing anxiety, learning to breathe deeply in moments of sheer terror. Growth happens out of those challenges.

I vow to be there when they need me most, just like how I stayed after school one day last year because one student was struggling with math. She looked so lost until she finally got those fractions—her face lit up like Christmas morning.

Insomnia’s a real thing when perfectionism hits. Night after night, staring at documents wishing they were just a tiny bit better. Promising to loosen up, allow imperfections, rest. Life beyond policy needs attention too.

I'll paint my nights away, every dark corner of the canvas a reflection of what I lost. Gonna make those colors scream until they match the silence inside me—because even if it hurts, it’s something.
I'm sick of waking up to negative comments before my morning coffee. I’m done pretending every “like” is validation for who I am. But if I stop posting? What do I have left?
I pledge to never leave a comrade behind, even if it means risking my own life. I saw too many fall on the drop zone; ain’t gonna let that happen again.
They try to pull rank and push around subordinates? I'm there telling 'em where to go. Not backing down when superiors get dictatorial. Gotta make sure the chain stays clean and everyone plays fair.
What if everyone hates my art? What if they see through me and my pretense? Do I have what it takes to keep creating something nobody cares about? Worthless, maybe?
Never let fear of failure stop you from trying again. It doesn’t matter how many times you fall short; what matters is getting up each morning ready to try harder next time.

When doubt creeps in, I’ll stare down that gremlin. It’s just noise anyway. My vision guides me, and I trust it more than those niggling fears. Art isn’t about perfection, it’s about expression, raw and real.
Telemedicine. I'm treating patients in villages I've never seen. The screen is a barrier, but I break through it with empathy. I'm the doctor in the pocket of the poor.

In cricket, silence speaks louder than words sometimes. Knowing when to speak up and when to listen is key. Respecting others’ space, their struggles, their battles. Cricket teaches empathy, understanding, unity. Together, we rise above the rest.

In the zone feels amazing, every twitch, every reaction firing perfectly. Then there are days when nothing goes right, and you feel like crap. Just gotta push through those lows, keep improving even when it sucks.

Can’t back down from the naysayers who think wind turbines are just another headache. They'll see we're making progress, helping the planet and people's lives. Keep fighting the good fight, even when it feels like nobody cares.

Promise myself to never fold under pressure, no matter how many times they knock me down. Every ball counts, every dropshot matters. The game is life, and if you're not fighting back, what's the point? Dig deep, push harder, find that spark inside that keeps burning, even when the scoreboard says otherwise.

In this biz, you gotta learn who owes you favors and who's just there to make trouble. Buildin' alliances is crucial, but don't stick your neck out too far. Got to navigate the waters smoothly, stayin' above board while still mixin’ in where needed.

When I look at charts filled with cryptic notes and codes, feeling like an outsider among fellow professionals, I swear to keep learning, staying updated on new research and techniques, never letting complacency creep into my practice ever again.

I'll keep pushing for change even when it feels like no one else is listening. Gotta grow uncomfortably to make real impact. Won't settle for easy wins or safe spaces. Each day I'm learning and adapting, building bridges between communities where none existed before.

Physical education teacher isn't real teacher, apparently. At 44, heard this my entire career. PE is filler period, sports are extra-curricular, physical fitness is luxury not necessity. This attitude is frustrating. Running school sports program for 15 years, watching children become sedentary, obese, physically weak. In the quiet moments post-match, reflecting on the day's play, swearing off the mistakes, embracing the lessons learned. Because every setback is just the setup for a comeback, waiting around the corner with a chance to reclaim what was lost.

Swear to God, I’ll do better next time around. No more screwing up cases due to stress and exhaustion—no more making mistakes cost lives or freedoms unnecessarily. Hope is what keeps us going, even after the worst losses.

Gonna stand firm against bureaucracy, pushing papers and begging for grants while juggling research and lectures. Sleep is optional when there’s paperwork piling up. If they want me to justify why we need more funding for coral reefs, I’ve got charts, stats, pictures—showing how bleaching kills off marine life. Gotta save those colorful corals somehow, even if it means pulling all-nighters.
Can I ever get this right? Every email, every proposal feels like a gamble. Will my boss think I’m incompetent? Do I need more training, more experience, or just less pressure? No idea.

Encounter killings happened under my watch. I was transferred before inquiry. I'm 39, in service 14 years.
